refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness (including tele-operation latency), and safety constraints that may impose timing overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Correlate with telemetry data (vehicle speed, acceleration, operator input latency, safety system activation events) to isolate performance bottlenecks.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time impact.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) for KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-2.1: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/electric actuation response time and travel speed within safety envelope; allocate cycle time budgets to load, travel, and dump sub-cycles; implement closed-loop control with predictive path planning.
DP-2.2: Design teleoperation network architecture with low-latency command/feedback channels (e.g., dedicated network link, edge computing, predictive control) to minimize operator-in-the-loop delay.

DP-2.3: Integrate safety services as non-blocking constraint enforcement (e.g., speed governors, geofence checks, collision avoidance) with pre-computed safety overhead budgets; avoid safety-driven cycle time penalties through design-time safety margins.

DP-2.4: Implement real-time telemetry pipeline (vehicle → network → backend) with cycle time event logging (cycle start/end, sub-cycle markers, latency samples, safety events) for post-hoc KPI analysis and continuous improvement.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Integrated approach: (1) Vehicle sub-system optimizes actuation and control to meet allocated cycle time budgets; (2) Teleoperation sub-system minimizes command latency via low-latency network link and predictive control; (3) Safety services enforce constraints with pre-budgeted overhead; (4) Telemetry pipeline captures cycle time events for KPI verification and root-cause analysis.

potential_sources: Vehicle cycle time budget depends on teleoperation latency allocation (if tele-operated) and safety constraint overhead—these are coupled design decisions.
Safety services and vehicle control must coordinate to avoid conflicting actuation commands (e.g., speed governor vs. operator demand).
Telemetry pipeline must not introduce feedback loops that degrade vehicle control stability or increase cycle time.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is manageable via explicit budget allocation (cycle time, latency, safety overhead) and interface contracts (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry). Recommend design-time trade-off analysis and simulation to validate feasibility.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target and duty cycle are formally defined and achievable within vehicle kinematic/dynamic limits.
Assumption: Teleoperation latency budget is compatible with operator control loop stability (typically ≤200 ms for heavy equipment).
Assumption: Safety constraint overhead (e.g., geofence checks, collision avoidance) can be pre-computed and does not exceed allocated safety_overhead_budget.
Risk: If CT_target is aggressive relative to vehicle capabilities, cycle time KPI may be unachievable without fundamental vehicle redesign.
Risk: Tele-operation latency spikes (network congestion, edge compute overload) could violate latency budget and degrade cycle time performance.
Risk: Safety services may introduce unpredictable delays (e.g., emergency stop) that violate cycle time SLA.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP to include explicit cycle time budget allocation table (load, travel, dump, safety overhead, teleoperation latency) and design-time feasibility gate. Add simulation/modeling requirement to validate that integrated DP achieves p95 ≤ CT_target before detailed design.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level integration module responsible for orchestrating vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services to achieve cycle time KPI. Owns cycle time budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and KPI reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits cycle time events (cycle start/end, sub-cycle durations, latency samples, safety interventions) to backend analytics for KPI verification and root-cause analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives load→travel→dump task commands from site manager or autonomous planner; translates to vehicle control and teleoperation directives.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety constraints/alerts from Safety Services (e.g., geofence, collision risk) and reports safety-driven cycle time impacts; coordinates safety enforcement with vehicle control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses network link for teleoperation command/feedback and telemetry transmission; monitors network latency and reports to telemetry pipeline.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural system-level owner of cycle time KPI orchestration, budget allocation, and telemetry aggregation. Minimal diffusion: cycle time logic is centralized at system level, with sub-system contributions (vehicle, teleoperation, safety) clearly scoped.

Vehicle owns actuation and control sub-cycles (load, travel, dump) but does not own system-level cycle time budget allocation, teleoperation latency management, or KPI reporting. Assigning cycle time KPI to Vehicle alone would diffuse responsibility for teleoperation and safety overhead.

Teleoperation owns command latency optimization but does not own vehicle actuation timing, safety constraint enforcement, or KPI aggregation. Assigning cycle time KPI to Teleoperation alone would obscure vehicle and safety contributions.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement but does not own vehicle control, teleoperation, or KPI reporting. Assigning cycle time KPI to Safety Services would diffuse responsibility for the primary cycle time drivers (vehicle and teleoperation).

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because it owns system-level cycle time orchestration, budget allocation across vehicle/teleoperation/safety sub-systems, and telemetry-based KPI verification. Sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) own their respective contributions but report cycle time metrics to NGHE Solution for aggregation.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution is too abstract (e.g., a logical orchestration layer without concrete implementation), cycle time ownership may diffuse to Vehicle and Teleoperation at implementation time.
Risk: Teleoperation and Safety Services may introduce unpredictable delays that violate cycle time SLA; NGHE Solution must have visibility and control over these latencies.
Risk: Telemetry pipeline may introduce feedback loops or processing delays that degrade vehicle control or inflate reported cycle times.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify NGHE Solution IM as a concrete system controller (e.g., vehicle ECU, edge compute node, or orchestration service) with explicit ownership of cycle time budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and KPI reporting. Define sub-system interfaces (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) with cycle time SLAs and latency budgets.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR (achieve p95 ≤ CT_target with telemetry verification) is directly addressed by selected DP (integrated optimization of vehicle, teleoperation, safety, and telemetry). DP is logically sound and feasible given explicit budget allocation and design-time trade-off analysis. No fundamental mismatch between problem and solution.

diffusion_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution IM is not concretely realized (e.g., remains a logical orchestration layer), cycle time budget allocation and telemetry aggregation may diffuse to Vehicle and Teleoperation at implementation time.
Risk: Teleoperation and Safety Services may introduce latencies not captured in design-time budgets; NGHE Solution must have runtime visibility and control mechanisms.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution IM is the appropriate owner of system-level cycle time orchestration and KPI reporting. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-system contributors with allocated budgets and SLAs. Containment is strong if NGHE Solution is realized as a concrete system controller with explicit budget allocation and telemetry aggregation logic.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is verifiable via telemetry (cycle start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency samples, safety events). Measurement requires instrumentation of vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services to emit cycle time events to NGHE Solution telemetry pipeline. Feasibility is high if telemetry infrastructure is in place.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time KPI is allocated to NGHE Solution with sub-allocations to Vehicle (load/travel/dump budgets), Teleoperation (latency budget), and Safety Services (overhead budget). Allocation is clear and traceable if budget allocation table is formalized in design documentation.

recommended_revisions: Formalize cycle time budget allocation table (load, travel, dump, safety overhead, teleoperation latency) with target values and acceptance criteria.
Define telemetry event schema (cycle start/end, sub-cycle markers, latency samples, safety events) to ensure consistent KPI measurement across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services.
Specify NGHE Solution IM as a concrete system controller (e.g., vehicle ECU, edge compute node) with explicit ownership of budget allocation and telemetry aggregation.
Add design-time feasibility gate: simulate integrated DP to validate that p95 ≤ CT_target is achievable before detailed design.

decision.justification: Refined FR and selected DP are logically sound and well-coupled. NGHE Solution IM is the appropriate system-level owner of cycle time KPI. However, the DP is complex and depends on sub-system contributions (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) that must be further decomposed to validate feasibility and allocate budgets. Recommend decomposing sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) to the next level to detail vehicle control, teleoperation latency optimization, safety constraint enforcement, and telemetry pipeline design.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time impact.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) for KPI verification.

assumptions: CT_target and duty cycle are formally defined and achievable within vehicle kinematic/dynamic limits.
Teleoperation latency budget is compatible with operator control loop stability (typically ≤200 ms for heavy equipment).
Safety constraint overhead can be pre-computed and does not exceed allocated safety_overhead_budget.
Telemetry infrastructure (event logging, aggregation, analytics) is available or will be developed as part of NGHE Solution.
NGHE Solution IM is realized as a concrete system controller (e.g., vehicle ECU, edge compute node) with explicit budget allocation and telemetry aggregation logic.
open_risks: Risk: CT_target may be aggressive relative to vehicle capabilities; feasibility must be validated via simulation before detailed design.
Risk: Tele-operation latency spikes (network congestion, edge compute overload) could violate latency budget and degrade cycle time performance.
Risk: Safety services may introduce unpredictable delays (e.g., emergency stop) that violate cycle time SLA.
Risk: Telemetry pipeline may introduce feedback loops or processing delays that degrade vehicle control or inflate reported cycle times.
Risk: If NGHE Solution IM is not concretely realized, cycle time ownership may diffuse to Vehicle and Teleoperation at implementation time.
evidence_needed: Formal definition of CT_target, duty cycle, and acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target).
Vehicle kinematic/dynamic model and simulation to validate that load/travel/dump budgets are achievable.
Teleoperation network architecture and latency analysis to validate that latency_budget is achievable.
Safety constraint analysis to quantify safety_overhead_budget and validate that safety services do not exceed budget.
Telemetry event schema and pipeline design to ensure consistent KPI measurement and reporting.
Design-time feasibility gate: simulation of integrated DP to validate that p95 ≤ CT_target is achievable before detailed design.
